items.content: To draft the weekly economics newsletter, I first identify 2-3 trending topics in African development economics relevant to my audience. Then, I research recent studies or news articles, synthesizing key findings into concise, accessible language. Finally, I structure the newsletter with an engaging introduction, distinct sections for each topic, and a clear call to action, ensuring it's sent out by Friday evening.

When onboarding a new smallholder cocoa farmer to the platform, I begin with a brief introductory call to explain the benefits and gather essential farm details. Next, I guide them through the app's registration process, ensuring accurate input of their farm size, location, and yield history. I then follow up within 48 hours to confirm their profile is active and answer any further questions.

For my weekly football practice, I always ensure my gear is packed the night before: boots, shin guards, and training kit. I arrive at the pitch 15 minutes early to warm up individually, focusing on stretching and light cardio. During practice, I actively participate in drills and communicate effectively with teammates to improve our coordination.

To prepare for a deacon's meeting at church, I review the agenda and gather any necessary reports or updates from my assigned ministry areas. I ensure I've prayed for guidance and wisdom regarding the discussions. During the meeting, I listen attentively, contribute thoughtfully to discussions, and take notes on action items assigned to me.

When developing a new feature for the agri-tech platform, I start by gathering user feedback and market research to define the core problem. I then create wireframes and mockups, iterating based on internal team reviews. Finally, I work with the development team to implement the feature, followed by rigorous testing and a phased rollout to farmers.
